ion sounds odd. He construes Akincandh (indigent)
as Akincitkardh puru$akdrahindh (LXVIII. 29). He explains the
word Slrimrtyufy (verse 61) as Stri tasya mdrqyati, which is evidently
wrong in grammar. The word Sraninibha (resembling hips) is
explained as StryadharardgasadrSam i.e. like the red tinge of a
woman's lower lip. Lavaliphala, according to him, is Lavanga-pufpam,
tasya phaldsambhavdt. Actually Lavafiga is clove» while lauali is Cicca
acida which is called Kirunelli in Kanna^a. Similarly he explains
the compound 'Vrl^avimisralasa' a bit clumsily by changing the
Kqrmadhdraya into Bahuvrihi. The word Sdkuni (fowler) is Dhlvarah
(fisherman) tor him', Avagdhana (plunging) is UI tar am (crossing);
(danger to ascetics) is changed to the ablative
case (i.e. danger from ascetics); the word Pdyu (anus) means
Upastha [goniia.l organ) ) Avalokayan ik$an (for xkiamdnah). Brahma-
varcah, Sragddmah, Ratnabheddni etc. are some other instances. In
